game_name: Déjà Vu: A Nightmare Comes True

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Game start - Ace wakes in Joe's Bar bathroom
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must locate and obtain antidote before memory deteriorates completely; finding cure triggers flashback memories that reveal puzzle solutions
Player gathers clues about murdered man and Ace's identity; interrogation artifacts (strap-down chair, vials, syringe, needle mark) provide narrative context
Player discovers addresses around Joe's Bar, uses taxi to travel to office and other locations to gather information

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Encounters with mugger and old acquaintance outside bar
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ace's boxing background becomes weapon for handling adversaries; some situations resolved through violence
Ace is being framed; must evade or navigate police encounters
Game requires significant lateral thinking beyond common detective techniques; some puzzles resist obvious solutions
Ace dies on second floor if visited before antidote administered (NES version removes this mechanic, instead triggering vegetable ending if office visited during amnesia)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mystery-driven progression with cognitive pressure; time-limited amnesia mechanic (original) creates urgency alongside puzzle-solving demands; solution discovery through interrogation and deduction reveals narrative and opens new locations

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Addresses discovered around Joe's Bar signal taxi-accessible optional locations for investigation
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Clues hidden in crime scene artifacts (strap-down chair, vials, syringe) require lateral thinking to interpret significance

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to amnesia mechanic and basic investigation in Joe's Bar; first adversary encounters teach combat; addresses discovery initiates taxi exploration phase
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Lateral thinking puzzles intensify; antidote search creates primary objective; multiple location investigation required; Sternwood Mansion presents hard gating with death consequence (original platforms)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Flashback memories unlock after antidote; evidence evaluation and connected puzzle-solving resolve kidnapping plot and reveal Ace's true role; narrative climax synthesis
notable_spikes: Antidote search urgency (time pressure on original platforms before vegetable status)
Sternwood Mansion location gating (instant death if visited pre-cure on original platforms)
Lateral thinking puzzle clusters requiring detective reasoning beyond violence
NES version removes time-pressure spike but adds hard-fail to office visit during amnesia

notable_level_patterns.description: Joe's Bar serves as starting location hub; player discovers addresses, explores immediate area for clues and artifacts, encounters initial adversaries; structures gate to taxi-based location traveling
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Joe's Bar and surrounding Chicago streets

notable_level_patterns.description: New locations become accessible through discovered addresses; player must gather evidence and clues to determine where to travel; taxi system bridges exploration

Lateral thinking puzzles block progress; solutions require connecting disparate clues (interrogation artifacts, discovered information, narrative hints); antidote cure and flashback memories provide unlock paths
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Antidote-hunt primary puzzle; kidnapping mystery resolution

notable_level_patterns.description: Sternwood Mansion presents instant-death consequence if entered before antidote (original platforms) or before curing amnesia effect (NES); represents story-critical gating mechanism
